name: Maple Syrup Brown Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 3/4 cups graham flour or whole wheat flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the two flours, salt, and baking soda in a large bowl.
2. In a separate bowl, combine the maple syrup with the buttermilk.
3. Pour the liquid mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
4. Stir in the raisins.
5. Grease a 1 1/2 quart mold and pour the batter into it.
6. Cover loosely with foil.
7. Place the mold in a steamer or on a trivet in a saucepan large enough to leave space around the mold.
8. Fill the pan with water to halfway up the mold.
9. Cover and steam over low heat for 3 1/2 hours, or until a skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.
Alternatively, bake at 325°F (163°C) for 1 hour, using two greased 4 x 8-inch bread pans.
10. Cool on a rack. Serve warm or reheat in the oven. This recipe freezes well; simply place the frozen loaf in a preheated 350°F (177°C) oven for 30 minutes or until hot.
